item.text: Batch 2 is confined to `OverlayEditor.tsx`. I’m updating the preview drag math to store composition-to-layout scale at drag start, then removing the intentional dashed `dragOriginStyle` layer that is causing the lingering ghost.

items.text: Inspect OverlayEditor drag state, drag math, and ghost rendering for T2/T3
Patch OverlayEditor to store drag-start scale and remove drag-origin ghost rendering
Run focused verification for SC2/SC3 and checkpoint batch progress

item.text: I have the two exact edit sites. The next patch adds drag-start `scaleX/scaleY` so cursor deltas are converted into composition space consistently, and deletes the dashed origin layer entirely so only the active overlay remains during drag.

The code changes are in. I’ve confirmed the drag ref now carries `scaleX/scaleY`, the mousemove path applies them before mutating bounds, and there are no remaining `dragOriginStyle` references in the JSX tree.

item.text: {"output":"Executed batch 2 of 6 for T2 and T3 only. Updated `src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx` so drag state now captures `scaleX`/`scaleY` at drag start and applies them to mouse deltas during move/resize, and removed the dashed `dragOriginStyle` origin layer that was causing the preview ghost artifact. Verified there are no remaining `dragOriginStyle` references in `OverlayEditor.tsx`, so the start-position ghost layer is no longer rendered during move or resize.","files_changed":["src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx"],"commands_run":["sed -n '140,340p' src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx","sed -n '200,320p' src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx","rg -n \"dragOriginStyle|startDrag\\(|scaleX|scaleY|dragState\" src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx","rg -n \"dragOriginStyle|scaleX|scaleY|startBounds: bounds|dragOverride\" src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx","nl -ba src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx | sed -n '44,70p;208,270p;296,312p'"]}],"sense_check_acknowledgments":[{"sense_check_id":"SC2","executor_note":"Confirmed by code inspection that drag deltas are now converted from screen pixels into composition units using `scaleX = compositionWidth / layout.width` and `scaleY = compositionHeight / layout.height`, captured in the drag ref at drag start and applied on every mousemove. item.text: The critical risk in this batch is silent persistence breakage, so I’m treating the two `timeline-data.ts` mapping sites and the `serialize.ts` whitelist as a single change set rather than piecemeal edits.

item.text: Batch 4 is the actual crop interaction layer. I’m reading the current overlay and render paths end-to-end first, because the crop drag math, displayed bounds, and Remotion `clip-path` need to agree on the same model or the preview will drift from the final render.

items.text: Inspect OverlayEditor and VisualClip to align preview crop math with rendered crop behavior
Patch OverlayEditor with crop-aware bounds helpers, edge handles, drag-state crop tracking, and crop commit logic
Patch VisualClip with CSS clip-path crop rendering, verify SC5/SC7, and checkpoint batch progress

item.text: I’ve got the crop geometry model pinned down: the editor will drag and render the visible cropped rectangle, while commits translate that visible rectangle back to full clip bounds so existing `x/y/width/height` semantics stay intact. The next patch wires that through `OverlayEditor` and adds the matching `clip-path` on the Remotion side.

The first pass hit a patch-context mismatch in `VisualClip.tsx`, so I’m re-reading the current file state before retrying. I haven’t moved on to any new work; this is just getting the edit aligned with the live file contents.

item.text: The core patch is in place. I’m verifying three things now: the crop branches are isolated from resize branches in `OverlayEditor`, the visible overlay bounds are derived from crop fractions, and `VisualClip` applies `clipPath: inset(top right bottom left)` so the render path matches the editor math.

I’m doing a compile-level sanity check next rather than guessing. This patch changes types and JSX structure in two files, so a quick TypeScript pass is the fastest way to catch any missed union or JSX closure issue before I checkpoint the batch.

item.text: The package manager guard rejected `pnpm`, so I’m rerunning the same typecheck with `npm exec`. That doesn’t change the scope of work; it just uses the repo’s configured toolchain.

I found one follow-on gap from the new crop model: a clip with crop but no explicit `x/y/width/height` would still be treated as having no overlay override unless it was already selected. I’m fixing that so crop itself counts as an overlay override and the cropped rectangle remains interactable.

The implementation is stable under `npm exec tsc --noEmit`, so I’m checkpointing this batch now. The checkpoint will capture both the interaction-side verification for crop handles/clamping and the render-side verification for `clip-path` ordering.

  "sense_check_acknowledgments": [
    {
      "sense_check_id": "SC5",
      "executor_note": "Confirmed from the patched `OverlayEditor` that edge handles render only under `!overlay.isText`, crop drags go through a dedicated `state.mode.startsWith('crop-')` branch instead of the resize path, and crop-mode commits update only crop fields. The clamp upper bounds use the opposite crop value plus a minimum visible fraction, so `cropLeft + cropRight` and `cropTop + cropBottom` cannot reach or exceed 1."
    },
    {
      "sense_check_id": "SC7",
      "executor_note": "Confirmed `VisualClip` now applies `style.clipPath = inset(cropTop cropRight cropBottom cropLeft)` in top-right-bottom-left order. That means `cropLeft = 0.1` produces a `10%` left inset, which removes 10% from the left side in Remotion preview/render. The code typechecks cleanly with `npm exec tsc --noEmit`."
    }
  ]
}

item.text: Batch 5 is a render-only refinement inside `OverlayEditor`. I’m wiring the selected cropped overlay to render a dimmed full-bounds ghost behind the visible cropped rectangle, using the crop-aware `fullBounds` model from the previous batch so this stays purposeful and doesn’t reintroduce the old drag-origin artifact.

items.text: Inspect the current crop-aware overlay rendering path in OverlayEditor
Patch OverlayEditor to render a selected cropped full-bounds ghost behind the visible overlay
Verify SC6 and checkpoint batch progress

item.text: The key constraint here is z-order and scope: the ghost should be the full uncropped area, but only for the selected cropped clip, and it needs to sit behind the actual cropped overlay rather than behave like the old always-on drag-origin box.

The patch context drifted from the live file, so I’m re-reading the exact `effectiveOverlays` and per-overlay render block before retrying. This is still the same batch work, just aligning the edit to the current file contents.

The ghost rendering is already in place in the live file: selected-only, gated by non-zero crop, and positioned from `fullBounds` relative to the visible cropped rectangle. I’m doing the final compile check and line-level verification now, then I’ll checkpoint batch 5.

item.text: Batch 6 is validation-only. I’m checking what can be proven in this environment with a mix of typecheck, targeted serialization round-trip exercises, and direct inspection of the reset/edit interaction paths, then I’ll separate confirmed behavior from anything that still needs manual UI verification.

The remaining validation splits cleanly into two buckets. I can prove persistence and reset behavior directly with a small `tsx` round-trip exercise plus code inspection, but the pointer-driven preview interactions and Remotion visual output still need manual UI confirmation, so I’m keeping those two classes of evidence separate in the batch result.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "npm exec tsx --eval \"import { configToRows, rowsToConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; const config = { output: { resolution: '1920x1080', fps: 30, file: 'out.mp4' }, tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'Track 1' }], clips: [{ id: 'clip1', at: 0, track: 'V1', asset: 'asset-1', from: 0, to: 2, cropTop: 0.1, cropRight: 0.2, cropBottom: 0.15, cropLeft: 0.05 }] }; const { rows, meta, clipOrder } = configToRows(config as any); const roundTripped = rowsToConfig(rows, meta, config.output as any, clipOrder, config.tracks as any); console.log(JSON.stringify({ meta: meta['clip1'], clip: roundTripped.clips[0] }, null, 2));\""
